sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All w pattern also didn't work. I notice something important - the stems are now different from the tops! After toggling all tops to w, the stems show B/w/B/w/B. This means the submit action somehow resets or changes the stems.
Let me think about this differently. The submit button seems to:
1. Change the stems back to B/w/B/w/B pattern
2. Not complete the level
Maybe the goal isn't about matching tops and stems. Maybe I need to look at the yellow U-shapes more carefully and figure out what pattern they represent.
Let me look at the bottom U-shape's bottom row (row 37). It has 6 yellow cells. If I think of the 5 buttons as representing positions, maybe the pattern should match which positions have yellow cells.
Actually, let me reconsider. The bottom U-shape at row 37 has Y at columns 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, 34. If the 5 buttons correspond to columns 29-33, then all 5 would be "on" (B). But that's what we just tried.
Wait, let me look at the top U-shape. At row 16, it has Y at cols 30 and 33 (the two "feet"). If buttons correspond to cols 29-33, then the pattern would be w/B/w/B/w (only positions 30 and 33 are B).
Let me try this pattern!

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- All B and all w patterns failed. Submit seems to reset stems to B/w/B/w/B.
- The pattern likely relates to the yellow U-shapes on the checkerboard.
- Top U-shape at row 16 has feet at cols 30 and 33. If buttons map to cols 29-33, pattern would be w/B/w/B/w.
- Let me try this pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("Current tops (row 42):")
row42 = lines[42]
for col in [21, 26, 31, 36, 41]:
    print(f"  col {col}: {row42[col]}")
print("Current stems (row 46):")
row46 = lines[46]
for col in [21, 26, 31, 36, 41]:
    print(f"  col {col}: {row46[col]}")

# Try w/B/w/B/w pattern (matching top U-shape feet positions)
# Current: w/w/w/w/w
# Need to toggle cols 26 and 36 to get w/B/w/B/w
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 26},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 36}
])

print("\nAfter setting pattern:")
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
row42 = lines[42]
for col in [21, 26, 31, 36, 41]:
    print(f"  col {col}: {row42[col]}")

# Submit
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 31}])
print("\nResult:", last_action_result)
print("Level:", current_frame.level)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
